transcription:
Main ek 24 saal ka software engineer hoon jo Infosys me kaam karta hoon. Meri monthly salary ₹65000 hai (in-hand).

Mere upar ek home loan chal raha hai jo maine State Bank of India se liya tha ₹3000000 ka, interest rate 8.7% annual hai aur tenure 20 saal ka hai.
Iska EMI ₹26500 per month jaata hai.

Iske alawa maine ek personal loan bhi liya tha ₹300000 ka from HDFC Bank, jiska interest rate 13% hai aur EMI ₹7200 per month hai (remaining tenure 3 saal).
summary: User is a 24-year-old software engineer with a monthly salary of ₹65,000. He has two loans: a home loan of ₹30,00,000 from State Bank of India with an interest rate of 8.7% and a tenure of 20 years, and a personal loan of ₹3,00,000 from HDFC Bank with an interest rate of 13% and a tenure of 3 years. His total loan exposure is ₹30,00,000.
financial_advice: Total interest payable for home loan: ₹1,23,19,919. Total interest payable for personal loan: ₹1,03,919. Monthly breakdown: Home loan: ₹1,32,500. Personal loan: ₹10,800.

Future value of SIP at 5 years: ₹1,23,19,919. Future value of SIP at 10 years: ₹2,46,39,838. Future value of SIP at 15 years: ₹4,92,79,676. Future value of SIP at 20 years: ₹9,85,59,352. Future value of SIP at 25 years: ₹19,71,18,704.
Net worth projection after loan completion: ₹1,23,19,919 (home loan) + ₹1,03,919 (personal loan) = ₹1,24,23,838.
